- Existing wasapi.rs unit tests pass on Windows (test_audio_device_supported_formats, test_default_device_returns_device)

(effort: medium | verify: windows-native | wave: 2)

title: Add AudioDevice::kind() fallible default + uniform DeviceKind across all three backends
description:
Extend the AudioDevice trait (src/core/interface.rs:41) with a NEW provided method fn kind(&self)->AudioResult<DeviceKind> defaulting to Err(AudioError::PlatformNotSupported{feature:'device kind',..}) (additive/non-breaking for external impls). Implement per backend: Windows already has inherent fallible WindowsAudioDevice::kind() (wasapi.rs:747 via IMMEndpoint::GetDataFlow) — trait method delegates (resolves the M1 api_fit clash by staying fallible). Linux LinuxAudioDevice (audio/linux/mod.rs:359) carries is_input/is_output — map to Input/Output (Input wins if both, documented). macOS MacosAudioDevice (coreaudio.rs:131) holds only device_id — probe scope via kAudioDevicePropertyStreams / scopeInput vs Output; Err if neither scope has streams. DeviceKind already exists/exported (interface.rs:19, lib.rs:107).

Acceptance criteria:
- AudioDevice gains provided kind()->AudioResult<DeviceKind> with a PlatformNotSupported default (no external-impl breakage)
- Windows trait kind() delegates to IMMEndpoint::GetDataFlow (Output for render, Input for capture)
- Linux kind() maps is_output->Output / is_input->Input with a documented tie-break
- macOS kind() probes CoreAudio stream scope returning Input/Output, or Err when indeterminate
- Per-platform unit test asserts the default device's kind() is Ok and matches the loopback expectation

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 2)

title: Add DeviceInfo snapshot + AudioDevice::describe() + additive AudioSourceKind::Device kind field
description:
Add pub struct DeviceInfo { pub id: DeviceId, pub name: String, pub kind: DeviceKind, pub is_default: bool, pub default_format: Option<AudioFormat> } (#[non_exhaustive]) in src/core/interface.rs, plus a provided AudioDevice::describe(&self)->DeviceInfo whose default composes id()/name()/is_default()/kind()/supported_formats().first(). Export DeviceInfo from lib.rs. Extend AudioSourceKind::Device (introspection.rs:42) ADDITIVELY with kind: Option<DeviceKind> (Option because Linux supported_formats is empty by design and kind probing can fail). Populate in list_audio_sources (introspection.rs:144) via dev.kind().ok(). Update the to_capture_target() match (introspection.rs:69) to ignore the new field. Doc-note that default_format is None on Linux by design (L2).

Acceptance criteria:
- DeviceInfo defined #[non_exhaustive] and re-exported from lib.rs
- AudioDevice::describe() default returns a DeviceInfo from existing trait methods; no backend must override it to compile
- AudioSourceKind::Device gains kind: Option<DeviceKind> without breaking to_capture_target() (the arm ignores the new field)
- list_audio_sources populates kind via dev.kind().ok() and existing introspection tests still pass
- Doc note records that default_format is None on Linux by design

Depends on: Add AudioDevice::kind() fallible default + uniform DeviceKind across all three backends

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 3)

title: macOS: enumerate all devices with multi-format probing and richer metadata
description:
MacosAudioDevice::supported_formats (coreaudio.rs:149) returns at most ONE format (single kAudioDevicePropertyStreamFormat query). Replace with per-stream probing: query kAudioDevicePropertyStreams for the output scope, then kAudioStreamPropertyAvailableVirtualFormats (or PhysicalFormats) per stream, converting each AudioStreamBasicDescription via the existing asbd_to_audio_format() (coreaudio.rs:342). De-duplicate. Gives macOS parity with the Windows multi-format list. MacosDeviceEnumerator::enumerate_devices (coreaudio.rs:240) already lists all device IDs — keep that; format probing is the real gap. Enumeration/init path only (RT-irrelevant).

Acceptance criteria:
- supported_formats returns >1 AudioFormat on a device advertising multiple virtual formats (probed via kAudioDevicePropertyStreams + AvailableVirtualFormats)
- Each probed ASBD is converted with the existing asbd_to_audio_format; non-LinearPCM/big-endian formats are skipped (not errored)
- Returns the current stream format first (stable ordering) and never panics on a zero-stream device (returns vec![])
- enumerate_devices continues to return all CoreAudio device IDs; a macOS test asserts the default output device reports >=1 format and supported_formats does not panic on any enumerated device

(effort: medium | verify: macos-ci | wave: 3)

title: macOS: filter enumerate_audio_applications to processes actually producing audio
description:
enumerate_audio_applications (coreaudio.rs:79) returns EVERY running NSWorkspace application, flooding list_audio_sources with non-audio processes. Add a CoreAudio-backed filter: enumerate audio process objects via kAudioHardwarePropertyProcessObjectList, keep only those with kAudioProcessPropertyIsRunning (or IsRunningInput/Output) true, intersecting against the NSWorkspace PID set to recover localizedName/bundleId. Keep an unfiltered enumerate_audio_applications_all() for diagnostics; make list_audio_applications_into (introspection.rs:175) consume the filtered set. macOS 14.4+ only (reuse get_macos_version gate); older macOS falls back to current behavior with a doc note.

Acceptance criteria:
- enumerate_audio_applications returns only PIDs with an active CoreAudio process object (IsRunning) on macOS 14.4+, each carrying name + bundle_id from NSWorkspace
- On macOS <14.4 it falls back to the existing NSWorkspace enumeration (documented)
- list_audio_sources no longer lists obviously non-audio background processes (assert count < raw NSWorkspace count, or a known non-audio helper is absent)
- No panic when the process object list property is unavailable (returns the NSWorkspace fallback)

(effort: medium | verify: macos-ci | wave: 3)

title: Add supports_device_change_notifications capability flag + honest macOS sub-14.4 fields
description:
Add three additive fields to PlatformCapabilities (src/core/capabilities.rs:31): supports_device_change_notifications: bool, min_os_for_application_capture: Option<&'static str>, system_capture_available_via: &'static str. Populate in windows()/linux()/macos()/unsupported(). Set supports_device_change_notifications: true on Windows/macOS (native IMMNotificationClient / AudioObjectPropertyListener exist); Linux false until the M10 watch arm lands. Add PlatformCapabilities::explain_unsupported(&self, target:&CaptureTarget)->Option<String> returning a hint (e.g. macOS-13 Application -> 'requires macOS 14.4+; ScreenCaptureKit assessed, backend not yet implemented'). Phrase SCK strictly as an assessment, never a usable route. Update the supports_format_missing test literal (~capabilities.rs:383) and every struct-literal construction to include the new fields.

Acceptance criteria:
- PlatformCapabilities gains the 3 fields; all four constructors and every struct-literal test compile
- supports_device_change_notifications is true on Windows + macOS; Linux false until M10 watch lands
- explain_unsupported returns Some(hint) for Application/ProcessTree on macOS <14.4 and None when supported; SCK phrased as assessed-not-implemented
- min_os_for_application_capture is Some('14.4') on macOS, None on Windows/Linux
- cargo test passes the capability unit tests on all platforms (including the updated literal)

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 2)

title: Linux: native in-process PipeWire registry enumeration (H4 part 1 — devices + default)
description:
Build the native registry path replacing pw-cli/pw-dump/pw-metadata for DEVICE and DEFAULT discovery. In src/audio/linux/thread.rs the loop creates MainLoop+Context+Core+Registry but binds let _registry (thread.rs:887) and never listens. Add registry + metadata listeners populating an Rc<RefCell<snapshot>> on the PipeWire thread (global callbacks are Fn+'static — use the wiremix Rc<RefCell> idiom). Add additive PipeWireCommand::SnapshotDevices{reply} and SnapshotDefault{reply}, and PipeWireThread::snapshot_devices()/snapshot_default() that MUST wait for a core.sync()/done roundtrip before replying (else it races an empty registry). Define PwDeviceSnapshot { id (object.serial), name, media_class }. Route LinuxDeviceEnumerator::enumerate_devices and default_device (audio/linux/mod.rs:458/493) through a short-lived PipeWireThread spawn instead of std::process::Command. Keep the subprocess path behind a cfg/fallback until proven on headless/Flatpak. Only owned Vecs cross the mpsc reply; callbacks fire on the PW loop thread, never the audio callback.

Acceptance criteria:
- New PipeWireCommand::SnapshotDevices/SnapshotDefault + PipeWireThread accessors exist; reply waits for a core sync/done roundtrip
- enumerate_devices returns audio Node ids/serials from the native registry with NO std::process::Command on the native path
- Returned device ids are numeric object.serial values that round-trip through CaptureTarget::Device (PwNodeLookup::Device contract, audio/linux/mod.rs:303)
- With pw-dump REMOVED from PATH but PipeWire running, enumerate_devices still returns devices
- Empty native result still surfaces BackendError{backend:'PipeWire'} (L4 honest-failure), never a fabricated default; cargo test --features feat_linux passes; a new test asserts non-empty snapshot when PipeWire is available

(effort: large | verify: wsl-feat_linux | wave: 2)

title: Linux: native in-process PipeWire application enumeration replacing pw-dump (H4 part 2)
description:
Replace the pw-dump subprocess in core/introspection.rs list_audio_applications_into (lines 209-268) with the native registry path from H4 part 1. Add PipeWireCommand::SnapshotApplications{reply} + PipeWireThread::snapshot_applications() returning PwAppSnapshot { pid, app_name, node_serial } built from registry Node globals whose props carry media.class containing 'Stream' and a parseable application.process.id (same predicate the subprocess parser uses at introspection.rs:215-247, but read from live registry props). Wire the Linux arm to call snapshot_applications via a short-lived discovery thread, dedup by PID exactly as today. Because introspection lives in core/ and must not depend on audio/ directly, route through the existing crate::audio facade (introspection.rs:137 calls crate::audio::get_device_enumerator — a sibling crate::audio::linux helper fn is the established precedent). Keep subprocess fallback behind cfg until proven.

Acceptance criteria:
- list_audio_applications() on Linux returns app sources from the native registry with NO pw-dump subprocess on the native path
- Same media.class contains 'Stream' + application.process.id predicate and PID-dedup as the subprocess parser
- With pw-dump removed from PATH but PipeWire running, list_audio_applications still returns running audio apps (not silently empty)
- core/ gains no direct dependency on audio/ internals — access via the crate::audio facade (DAG preserved)
- Existing introspection tests pass; a new test exercises the native app snapshot path when PipeWire is available

Depends on: Linux: native in-process PipeWire registry enumeration (H4 part 1 — devices + default)

(effort: large | verify: wsl-feat_linux | wave: 3)

title: Linux: wire supported_formats() via PipeWire node enum_params (PR-5)
description:
With H4 landing the registry/proxy machinery, implement Linux supported_formats() as real format DISCOVERY (not conversion — in capture scope). For a LinuxAudioDevice id (object.serial), bind the Node proxy on the PipeWire thread and call enum_params for SPA_PARAM_EnumFormat, translating each spa audio format/rate/channel set to rsac AudioFormat. Add PipeWireCommand::EnumNodeFormats{serial,reply} + accessor; reply after a sync/done roundtrip. CRITICAL (EF-3 rejection / L2): this must NOT override the authoritative connect-time negotiation reported via param_changed — supported_formats is advisory discovery only; the delivered buffer's format remains whatever PipeWire negotiates. If enum_params yields nothing, return vec![] (preserving documented-empty behavior at audio/linux/mod.rs:373) rather than guessing. Update the L2 doc comment (audio/linux/mod.rs:373-382).

Acceptance criteria:
- supported_formats returns formats discovered via SPA_PARAM_EnumFormat for nodes that advertise them, else vec![] (never a fabricated guess)
- New PipeWireCommand::EnumNodeFormats + accessor wait for a sync/done roundtrip
- Connect-time param_changed negotiation remains authoritative — a test or doc assertion confirms supported_formats is advisory and does not alter delivered AudioBuffer.format()
- L2 doc comment updated to describe advisory discovery vs authoritative negotiation
- cargo test --features feat_linux: the supported_formats-empty fallback test still passes when enum_params yields nothing

Depends on: Linux: native in-process PipeWire registry enumeration (H4 part 1 — devices + default)

(effort: medium | verify: wsl-feat_linux | wave: 4)

title: Add DeviceEvent + DeviceWatcher types and DeviceEnumerator::watch() default (M10 surface)
description:
Define the public hotplug/default-change surface in src/core/interface.rs: pub enum DeviceEvent { DeviceAdded{id,name,kind}, DeviceRemoved{id}, DefaultChanged{id,kind}, StateChanged{id,available} } (#[non_exhaustive]), and pub struct DeviceWatcher (RAII; Drop unregisters the OS listener + joins the notify thread). Add a PROVIDED trait method DeviceEnumerator::watch(&self, on_event: Box<dyn FnMut(DeviceEvent)+Send+'static>)->AudioResult<DeviceWatcher> defaulting to Err(PlatformNotSupported) (additive for external impls). Add an inherent CrossPlatformDeviceEnumerator::watch(...) (audio/mod.rs:62) that dispatches to per-backend impls (this inherent method is the real public entry — CrossPlatformDeviceEnumerator does not impl the trait). Export DeviceEvent + DeviceWatcher from lib.rs. Types + plumbing only; per-OS bodies are follow-on tasks. Unimplemented arms return PlatformNotSupported, consistent with supports_device_change_notifications=false.

Acceptance criteria:
- DeviceEvent (#[non_exhaustive]) + DeviceWatcher defined and exported from lib.rs
- DeviceEnumerator gains a provided watch() returning Err(PlatformNotSupported) by default (no external-impl breakage)
- CrossPlatformDeviceEnumerator::watch inherent method exists and dispatches per backend; unimplemented backends return PlatformNotSupported
- DeviceWatcher's Drop is documented to unregister the OS listener and join the notify thread; handler documented to run on the OS/PipeWire notify thread, never the audio callback
- cargo check on all three platforms compiles

Depends on: Add supports_device_change_notifications capability flag + honest macOS sub-14.4 fields

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 3)

title: Windows: implement DeviceEnumerator::watch() via IMMNotificationClient (M10 Windows arm)
description:
Implement WindowsDeviceEnumerator::watch using RegisterEndpointNotificationCallback + an IMMNotificationClient COM callback (wasapi.rs, alongside WindowsDeviceEnumerator at line 790). Map OnDeviceAdded/OnDeviceRemoved/OnDefaultDeviceChanged/OnDeviceStateChanged to DeviceEvent. Deadlock-safety (cpal/CamillaDSP pattern): the COM callback must NOT invoke the user FnMut directly on the notification thread in a way that can deadlock COM — push a DeviceEvent into a bounded channel and run the user FnMut on a dedicated notify thread owned by DeviceWatcher; Drop calls UnregisterEndpointNotificationCallback and joins. WASAPI notification thread, not the audio callback — no allocation/lock policy violation.

Acceptance criteria:
- WindowsDeviceEnumerator::watch registers an IMMNotificationClient and returns a DeviceWatcher
- OnDefaultDeviceChanged/OnDeviceAdded/OnDeviceRemoved/OnDeviceStateChanged translate to the matching DeviceEvent variants delivered to the user FnMut on a non-COM-notification helper thread (bounded-channel hand-off)
- DeviceWatcher::drop calls UnregisterEndpointNotificationCallback and joins the notify thread with no leak/hang
- Windows integration test: register a watcher, toggle the default device (or assert no crash over a short live window), confirm clean drop
- supports_device_change_notifications remains true on Windows

Depends on: Add DeviceEvent + DeviceWatcher types and DeviceEnumerator::watch() default (M10 surface)

(effort: large | verify: windows-native | wave: 5)

title: macOS: implement DeviceEnumerator::watch() via AudioObjectPropertyListener (M10 macOS arm)
description:
Implement MacosDeviceEnumerator::watch (coreaudio.rs:239) by registering AudioObjectAddPropertyListener on kAudioObjectSystemObject for kAudioHardwarePropertyDevices (add/remove) and kAudioHardwarePropertyDefaultOutputDevice / DefaultInputDevice (DefaultChanged). On each callback, diff the current device-id set against the previous snapshot to emit DeviceAdded/DeviceRemoved, and emit DefaultChanged with the new default's id + kind. The listener runs on a CoreAudio-managed thread; forward DeviceEvent to the user FnMut via a bounded-channel + helper thread hand-off (matching the Windows arm). DeviceWatcher::drop must AudioObjectRemovePropertyListener for every registered address (RAII). Reuse asbd/kind probing from the kind() task to populate DeviceAdded.kind.

Acceptance criteria:
- MacosDeviceEnumerator::watch registers listeners for device-list and default-output/input changes and returns a DeviceWatcher
- Adding/removing an aggregate or virtual device (e.g. BlackHole) emits DeviceAdded/DeviceRemoved; changing the default emits DefaultChanged with id + kind
- DeviceWatcher::drop removes every registered property listener (no dangling listener, no leak)
- macOS integration test: register a watcher and assert clean registration + drop over a short window without panic
- DeviceAdded.kind is populated via the shared kind-probing helper

Depends on: Add DeviceEvent + DeviceWatcher types and DeviceEnumerator::watch() default (M10 surface); Add AudioDevice::kind() fallible default + uniform DeviceKind across all three backends

(effort: large | verify: macos-ci | wave: 5)

title: Linux: implement DeviceEnumerator::watch() via persistent PipeWire registry/metadata listener (M10 Linux arm)
description:
Implement the Linux watch arm on top of the H4 native registry infrastructure. LinuxDeviceEnumerator is a unit struct with no persistent loop, so watch() must spawn a NEW persistent PipeWire main-loop + registry + metadata listener thread (distinct from the per-call snapshot threads in H4). Registry global/global_remove callbacks -> DeviceAdded/DeviceRemoved (filtered to audio Node media.class); the default.audio.sink/source Metadata listener -> DefaultChanged. Forward DeviceEvent to the user FnMut. DeviceWatcher::drop signals the loop to quit and joins (RAII). Callbacks fire on the PW loop thread (Fn+'static, Rc<RefCell> idiom), never the audio callback. Flip supports_device_change_notifications to true for Linux in capabilities.rs::linux() ONLY in this task.

Acceptance criteria:
- LinuxDeviceEnumerator::watch spawns a persistent pw main-loop+registry+metadata listener thread and returns a DeviceWatcher
- Registry add/remove of audio Nodes emit DeviceAdded/DeviceRemoved; default.audio.sink/source metadata changes emit DefaultChanged
- DeviceWatcher::drop quits the loop and joins the thread (no leaked thread, no hang)
- capabilities.rs::linux() now sets supports_device_change_notifications: true
- WSL/Linux test with PipeWire available: register a watcher, load/unload a null sink (pactl), observe DeviceAdded/DeviceRemoved; clean drop

Depends on: Add DeviceEvent + DeviceWatcher types and DeviceEnumerator::watch() default (M10 surface); Linux: native in-process PipeWire registry enumeration (H4 part 1 — devices + default)

(effort: large | verify: wsl-feat_linux | wave: 5)

title: Add a cross-platform enumeration non-empty / honest-failure test matrix + DeviceInfo round-trip
description:
Lock in the enumeration contract (audit L4). Add a tests/ci_audio module (or extend tests/ci_audio/main.rs) that, per platform with audio available: (a) asserts get_device_enumerator().enumerate_devices() is Ok(non-empty) OR the documented honest BackendError — never Ok(empty), never a fabricated 'default' id; (b) asserts every returned device's describe() yields a DeviceInfo whose to_capture_target() round-trips (DeviceId resolvable back through CaptureTarget::Device); (c) asserts kind() is Ok on the default device; (d) on Linux, asserts the native path works with pw-dump removed from PATH. Provide a shared assert_enumeration_honest!() macro so each backend's test is one line. Inspection/CI gates, not product code.

Acceptance criteria:
- A shared assert_enumeration_honest! macro asserts non-empty-or-honest-BackendError and no fabricated default id, used by all three backend tests
- DeviceInfo round-trip test: describe().to_capture_target() yields a CaptureTarget::Device whose id matches the source device id for every enumerated device
- default device kind() returns Ok in the per-platform test
- Linux test variant runs with pw-dump removed from PATH and still passes via the native registry path
- Tests are wired into the ci_audio harness and skip cleanly (eprintln) when no audio stack is present (matching pipewire_available() gating)

Depends on: Add DeviceInfo snapshot + AudioDevice::describe() + additive AudioSourceKind::Device kind field; Linux: native in-process PipeWire registry enumeration (H4 part 1 — devices + default)

(effort: medium | verify: wsl-feat_linux | wave: 4)

title: Bump transitive enumeration deps and remove serde_json from the native Linux path
description:
Two parts. (1) Dep hygiene: confirm sysinfo (0.38.4) and serde_json (1.0.149, used ONLY to parse pw-dump JSON in introspection.rs:213 and bin/) are current; bump to latest compatible patch and re-run the suite. (2) Once H4 parts 1+2 land, serde_json is no longer needed on the native Linux enumeration path — remove the pw-dump JSON parse once the subprocess fallback is retired, or scope serde_json to dev/diagnostics-only if still used by bin/pipewire_diagnostics. Gated behind H4 so the fallback is deleted only after native is proven.

Acceptance criteria:
- sysinfo and serde_json pinned to latest compatible versions; cargo update reflected in Cargo.lock; suite passes on all platforms
- After H4 lands, the pw-dump JSON parse in introspection.rs is removed (or the subprocess fallback is cfg-gated) and serde_json is no longer pulled into the default Linux library build (verify cargo tree --features feat_linux)
- If serde_json remains only for bin/pipewire_diagnostics, it is moved out of the library's hard dependency set
- No new clippy warnings on the 1.95.0 toolchain

Depends on: Linux: native in-process PipeWire application enumeration replacing pw-dump (H4 part 2)

(effort: small | verify: wsl-feat_linux | wave: 5)

title: Define target-conditional feature presets shared across rsac-ffi/rsac-napi/rsac-python
description:
Establish one feature-resolution pattern for all binding Cargo.toml files: each depends on rsac with default-features=false and selects the host backend via [target.'cfg(...)'.dependencies.rsac] blocks (feat_windows on windows, feat_linux on linux, feat_macos on macos), plus passthrough sink-wav/async-stream where exposed. rsac-ffi already mirrors features manually (rsac-ffi/Cargo.toml:13-19) but rsac-napi (rsac-napi/Cargo.toml:13 pulls implicit default = all three backends, bloating non-host builds) and rsac-python (hardcoded feat_macos) diverge. Document the convention in bindings/README or AGENTS so future bindings copy it. Grounding for the publishability + cross-compile tasks.

Acceptance criteria:
- rsac-ffi, rsac-napi, rsac-python each compile only the host platform's backend by default (a Linux build does not pull in windows/coreaudio crates)
- An explicit opt-in (e.g. --features all-backends) still exists where docs.rs-style all-platform compile is needed
- Convention documented in one place referenced by all three manifests

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 2)

title: Make rsac-ffi publishable to crates.io
description:
In bindings/rsac-ffi/Cargo.toml remove publish=false (line 7) and add crates.io-required metadata (repository, readme, keywords, categories) mirroring the root Cargo.toml. The rsac path dependency (line 22) must gain a version requirement (rsac = { path='../../', version='0.2', default-features=false }) so cargo publish resolves against the published crate. Adopt the target-conditional default features (feature-preset task) so a bare build/publish compiles the host backend. Add rsac-ffi to the release.yml publish job AFTER the rsac crate publishes (workflow currently publishes only the root crate per release.yml:14-23).

Acceptance criteria:
- cargo publish --dry-run -p rsac-ffi succeeds (no publish=false, all required metadata present)
- rsac dependency carries both path and version so it resolves post-publish
- release.yml publishes rsac-ffi as a step gated on the rsac crate publish succeeding first
- include/rsac.h is included in the published package (verify package contents in dry-run output)

Depends on: Define target-conditional feature presets shared across rsac-ffi/rsac-napi/rsac-python

(effort: medium | verify: inspection | wave: 3)

title: Eliminate f64 expansion in NAPI AudioChunk: expose interleaved f32 via Float32Array
description:
In bindings/rsac-napi/src/lib.rs the AudioChunk.data field is Vec<f64> (line 39) populated by buf.data().iter().map(|&s| s as f64).collect() (line 56) — a per-buffer allocation + f32->f64 doubling on the delivery path (start_data_pump at lib.rs:478 and the read* methods). Change the JS-facing representation to carry f32 directly: replace the #[napi(object)] struct field with a napi Float32Array/TypedArray<f32> (or Buffer over the f32 byte view) built from buf.data() so JS receives a native Float32Array with no precision-widening copy. Update index.d.ts. Keep num_frames/channels/sample_rate/length/duration. Note the type change in CHANGELOG and bump NAPI minor.

Acceptance criteria:
- AudioChunk no longer allocates a Vec<f64>; JS receives a Float32Array (or f32-backed Buffer) over the captured samples
- node --test passes: a captured chunk's data.length == num_frames*channels and values match the source f32 within exact equality
- index.d.ts updated to reflect Float32Array; README example still compiles
- No f32->f64 conversion remains in from_rsac_buffer

(effort: medium | verify: inspection | wave: 2)

title: Fix #30: replace unaligned f32->u8 reinterpret in Python to_bytes() with a sound conversion
description:
In bindings/rsac-python/src/lib.rs the to_bytes() method (lines 316-322) does std::slice::from_raw_parts(data.as_ptr() as *const u8, data.len()*4). While &[f32]->&[u8] is alignment-safe (u8 align=1), the pattern is the flagged #30 align_to concern and is fragile if copy-pasted into the reverse direction. Replace with a provably-sound conversion: depend on bytemuck and use bytemuck::cast_slice::<f32,u8>(data) (preferred — zero-copy, statically sound, removes the unsafe block), or build via data.iter().flat_map(|s| s.to_le_bytes()). Document the little-endian f32 contract in the docstring.

Acceptance criteria:
- to_bytes() contains no hand-rolled from_raw_parts reinterpret; uses bytemuck::cast_slice or an explicitly-safe path
- Returned bytes length == len()*4 and round-trips: numpy.frombuffer(b, dtype='<f4') equals to_list()
- cargo build -p rsac-python and the Python smoke test pass; no clippy warning on the conversion
- GitHub issue #30 referenced in the commit/PR and closed

(effort: small | verify: go-build | wave: 2)

title: Fix #28: harden Go callback path against use-after-free of the C buffer pointer
description:
Audit and lock down the #28 Go UAF in bindings/rsac-go/callback.go and rsac.go. goAudioCallback (callback.go:24-65) already copies C f32 data into Go memory before invoking the user fn — good — but verify two hazards: (1) the cgo.Handle lifecycle in SetCallback/clearCallbackHandleLocked (callback.go:91-110) must guarantee the handle is never resolved after Delete() during a concurrent in-flight callback (a callback firing on the FFI delivery thread while Close() runs). Introduce ordering so rsac_capture_set_callback(NULL)/stop is issued and drained BEFORE the cgo.Handle is Deleted, or guard resolution. (2) audioBufferFromC (rsac.go:281-313) copies then frees the C buffer via defer — confirm no Go AudioBuffer aliases the C memory after free. Add a stress test registering a callback and start/stop/closing rapidly under -race.

Acceptance criteria:
- Callback delete ordering ensures no cgo.Handle.Value() resolves after Delete() (clear-callback at the C layer precedes handle Delete)
- go test -race of a start/callback/stop/close churn test passes with no race or UAF report
- audioBufferFromC verified to leave no Go slice aliasing freed C memory (data is always make()+copy)
- GitHub issue #28 referenced and closed

(effort: medium | verify: inspection | wave: 2)

title: Add FFI stats accessors: rsac_capture_stream_stats() + rsac_capture_format()
description:
Once AudioCapture::stream_stats()/format() exist, expose them through the C ABI in bindings/rsac-ffi/src/lib.rs. Add a #[repr(C)] struct rsac_stream_stats_t { overruns:u64, buffers_captured:u64, buffers_dropped:u64, uptime_secs:f64, is_running:u8 } and rsac_capture_stream_stats(capture:*const RsacCapture, out:*mut rsac_stream_stats_t)->rsac_error_t (null-checked, catch()-wrapped like the existing functions at lib.rs:484+). Add rsac_capture_format(capture, *mut rsac_audio_format_t) mirroring AudioFormat (sample_rate u32, channels u16, sample_format enum). Regenerate include/rsac_generated.h (build.rs:7-16 runs cbindgen) and hand-mirror into include/rsac.h. ABI additive — only new symbols.

Acceptance criteria:
- New C functions compile, are catch()-guarded and null-checked, and appear in the regenerated rsac_generated.h
- rsac_capture_stream_stats returns overruns matching rsac_capture_overrun_count(); buffers_dropped/uptime are populated
- include/rsac.h hand-mirror updated in lockstep (the Go binding's ABI source)
- Existing FFI symbols unchanged (no ABI break)

Depends on: Implement AudioCapture::stream_stats() and complete the StreamStats struct (H3)

(effort: medium | verify: go-build | wave: 3)

title: Fix #29: SHA-pin all third-party GitHub Actions in ci.yml
description:
ci.yml pins actions by floating tag (actions/checkout@v5, Swatinem/rust-cache@v2, taiki-e/install-action@v2 — ci.yml:46,50,370 etc.), a supply-chain risk. release.yml already pins by full 40-char SHA with a version comment (release.yml:82-86). Apply the same convention to ci.yml: replace every uses: <action>@v* with uses: <action>@<full-sha> # vX for all third-party actions (first-party actions/* may follow the same policy for consistency). Reuse the exact SHAs already in release.yml where the same action+version is used.

Acceptance criteria:
- Every third-party uses: in ci.yml is pinned to a full 40-hex SHA with a trailing # vX comment
- SHAs for shared actions match those already pinned in release.yml
- ci.yml still parses and the workflow runs green on a no-op push
- GitHub issue #29 referenced and closed

(effort: small | verify: inspection | wave: 2)

title: Surface stream_stats()/format() in Python, Node, and Go bindings
description:
Propagate the stats/format facade to the higher-level bindings so downstreams stop polling only overrun_count. Python (rsac-python/src/lib.rs): add stream_stats() returning a frozen PyStreamStats pyclass (overruns, buffers_captured, buffers_dropped, uptime_secs, is_running) and a format getter returning a namedtuple (sample_rate, channels, sample_format) on PyAudioCapture (near overrun_count at lib.rs:660). Node (rsac-napi/src/lib.rs): add streamStats(): StreamStats (#[napi(object)] JsStreamStats) and a format getter, next to overrun_count at lib.rs:461. Go (rsac.go): add (c *AudioCapture) StreamStats()(StreamStats,error) and Format()(Format,error) calling the new FFI functions, mirroring OverrunCount at rsac.go:541. Update each binding's .pyi/.d.ts/doc.

Acceptance criteria:
- Python: cap.stream_stats() returns overruns/buffers_captured/buffers_dropped/uptime_secs/is_running; cap.format returns sample_rate/channels/sample_format; .pyi updated
- Node: capture.streamStats() and capture.format exist and are typed in index.d.ts; node --test asserts fields present
- Go: StreamStats()/Format() return populated structs; go test asserts OverrunCount() == StreamStats().Overruns
- All three build against the new FFI/Rust symbols with no ABI mismatch

Depends on: Add FFI stats accessors: rsac_capture_stream_stats() + rsac_capture_format()

(effort: medium | verify: inspection | wave: 4)

title: Add string-target convenience to all bindings via CaptureTarget::FromStr
description:
With core FromStr landed (H1), give each binding a single string entry point so downstreams stop hand-rolling target parsing. FFI: add rsac_builder_set_target_str(builder, *const c_char)->rsac_error_t in rsac-ffi/src/lib.rs parsing via target.parse::<CaptureTarget>() and mapping a parse error to RSAC_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETER (alongside typed setters at lib.rs:287-412). NAPI: add CaptureTarget.parse(s: string): CaptureTarget factory (rsac-napi/src/lib.rs:95), throwing on parse error. Python: add CaptureTarget.parse(s) staticmethod (rsac-python/src/lib.rs:187) raising ConfigurationError on failure. Go: add ParseTarget(s string)(CaptureTarget,error) and CaptureBuilder.WithTargetString(s) (rsac.go:343) deferring to rsac_builder_set_target_str. Keep all existing typed constructors (additive).

Acceptance criteria:
- Each binding round-trips the grammar (pid:1234, name:Firefox, device:..., app:..., system) into the correct target
- Invalid strings surface the binding-idiomatic error (RSAC_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETER / thrown error / Go error) without panicking or aborting
- Existing typed target constructors remain and still work (no removals)
- Per-binding test covers one valid and one invalid parse

Depends on: Implement CaptureTarget FromStr + TryFrom<&str> + Display round-trip (H1)

(effort: medium | verify: inspection | wave: 4)

title: Expose AudioBuffer metering through bindings (Python/Node/Go)
description:
With core AudioBuffer metering landed (H2), delete the duplicated hand-rolled rms/peak in rsac-python/src/lib.rs:335-353 (which re-iterates samples) and call the new core methods; add rms()/peak()/rms_dbfs()/peak_dbfs() to NAPI AudioChunk and Go AudioBuffer (rsac.go:232 methods block) so all bindings get metering for free. Capture-only (read-only measurement). Update each binding's .pyi/.d.ts/doc.

Acceptance criteria:
- rsac-python no longer hand-rolls rms/peak (lib.rs:335-353 removed in favor of core); the Python meter values match the core methods
- Node AudioChunk and Go AudioBuffer expose rms/peak/rms_dbfs/peak_dbfs typed in index.d.ts / Go signatures
- Per-binding test asserts metering on a known buffer matches expected values
- All three bindings build

Depends on: Add zero-alloc AudioBuffer metering: rms/peak/dBFS, channel-strided, NaN-safe (H2)

(effort: small | verify: inspection | wave: 4)

title: Establish a versioning + ABI-change contract across rsac and the four bindings
description:
Bindings version independently with no breakage tracking (rsac 0.2.0, rsac-ffi 0.1.0, rsac-python 0.2.0, rsac-napi 0.2.0, rsac-go has no version field — go.mod:1-3). Define and document the policy in docs/RELEASE_PROCESS.md (referenced by release.yml:1-23): (a) all crates bump in lockstep on a semver tag; (b) any change to the C ABI (rsac-ffi exported symbols / rsac.h) is a MAJOR for rsac-ffi and requires a CHANGELOG ABI section; (c) tag rsac-go releases (git tags bindings/rsac-go/vX.Y.Z) and record the module path. Add an ABI-changes subsection to CHANGELOG.md and a CI check (extend scripts/bump-version.sh) asserting all four binding manifests carry the same version on a release tag.

Acceptance criteria:
- RELEASE_PROCESS.md documents lockstep bump, ABI-major rule for rsac-ffi, and rsac-go tag convention
- CHANGELOG.md has an explicit 'C ABI changes' subsection format
- A CI/script check fails if rsac-ffi/rsac-python/rsac-napi versions diverge from the release tag
- rsac-go release tagging documented (module path + tag shape)

(effort: small | verify: inspection | wave: 2)

title: Build PyPI wheels for rsac-python across the platform matrix (cibuildwheel/maturin, abi3-py39)
description:
Wire bindings/rsac-python into a publishable wheel matrix. The existing release-pypi.yml + pyproject.toml (maturin backend, requires-python >=3.9) lack a multi-platform wheel build. Add maturin abi3 (features=['pyo3/abi3-py39'] in pyproject [tool.maturin]) so one wheel per platform covers 3.9+ (rsac#18). Use maturin-action / cibuildwheel to build manylinux (x86_64+aarch64, with PipeWire dev libs in the container), Windows x86_64, and macOS universal2/arm64 wheels + sdist, then publish via PyPI Trusted Publishing (OIDC, no token). Each platform wheel compiles only its backend (depends on the feature-preset task).

Acceptance criteria:
- release-pypi.yml builds wheels for linux (manylinux x86_64+aarch64), windows x86_64, macos arm64/universal2, plus an sdist
- abi3-py39 produces a single wheel per platform usable on CPython 3.9-3.13 (verified by importing on >1 minor version)
- manylinux build installs PipeWire dev deps so the linux wheel links feat_linux
- Dry-run publish via Trusted Publishing succeeds (artifacts uploaded to the workflow, no long-lived token)

Depends on: Fix rsac-python feature hygiene: target-conditional backends instead of hardcoded feat_macos; Define target-conditional feature presets shared across rsac-ffi/rsac-napi/rsac-python

(effort: large | verify: macos-ci | wave: 6)

title: Route binding crates through rsac::prelude
description:
Update the binding crates to import from rsac::prelude where it reduces the flat import lists (rsac-ffi/src/lib.rs:24-27 imports 7 names, rsac-napi, rsac-python). Purely additive — keep the existing top-level re-exports for back-compat. At least the FFI crate's import block uses the prelude.

Acceptance criteria:
- At least the FFI crate's import block uses use rsac::prelude::*; and compiles
- Existing binding behavior unchanged (no symbol removed)
- cargo build of each touched binding passes

Depends on: Add rsac::prelude module with explicit re-exports

(effort: small | verify: go-build | wave: 3)

title: Add a cross-compilation build matrix + build instructions for rsac-go's static FFI lib
description:
rsac-go links librsac_ffi.a built by the Makefile (rsac-go/Makefile:62-77) but there is no platform build matrix, the bundled lib/librsac_ffi.a is single-target, and example_test.go is incomplete. Add a CI matrix (or document + script) that builds rsac-ffi as a staticlib for linux-gnu (x86_64+aarch64), windows-gnu (so cgo's MinGW links the GNU .a — Makefile:39-46), and macOS arm64, then runs go test (test-pure target at Makefile:99-103 for no-device CI). Document per-platform prerequisites (PipeWire dev libs on Linux, frameworks on macOS, *-pc-windows-gnu on Windows) in rsac-go/README. Do NOT commit prebuilt .a binaries per-platform into git; build in CI.

Acceptance criteria:
- CI builds librsac_ffi.a for linux x86_64+aarch64, windows-gnu, macos arm64 and runs make test-pure green on each
- rsac-go/README documents per-platform prerequisites and the windows-gnu cgo requirement
- go vet / go build ./... succeeds on each platform in CI
- example_test.go compiles (completed or marked //go:build ignore with a runnable example documented)

Depends on: Define target-conditional feature presets shared across rsac-ffi/rsac-napi/rsac-python

(effort: large | verify: inspection | wave: 6)

title: Add Python async context-manager cleanup + finalizer safety net
description: rsac-python: add async context-manager (__aenter__/__aexit__) cleanup and a __del__ finalizer safety net so a dropped/abandoned Python AudioCapture deterministically stops the stream and frees the FFI handle, mirroring the Rust Drop semantics. Grounded in bindings survey + downstream ergonomics. (effort: small | verify: inspection | wave: 4)
